Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I like this patch, but I don't understand why you did not remove ldlm_inode_from_lock() and/or just change the ldlm_inode_from_lock() function to return the inode from the resource?  Having the old function around adds to confusion for other developers. 

I think ldlm_inode_from_lock() is actually a better name for this function, since it takes a lock as the parameter, and not a resource. The fact that the inode is now stored in the resource is an internal detail that should be hidden from the caller.

Patch Set 1:

>like this patch, but I don't understand why you did not remove >ldlm_inode_from_lock() and/or just change the ldlm_inode_from_lock() >function to return the inode from the resource?  Having the old function >around adds to confusion for other developers. 

>I think ldlm_inode_from_lock() is actually a better name for this function, >since it takes a lock as the parameter, and not a resource. The fact that the >inode is now stored in the resource is an internal detail that should be >hidden from the caller.

Unfortunately we cannot change osc part so easily as CLIO stores osc-lock pointer there instead of inode, see osc_lock_build_einfo
    einfo->ei_cbdata = lock; /* value to be put into ->l_ast_data */

llu part which handles extent locks still uses inodes, calling lov/osc directly, not through CLIO, however osc part is common for CLIO and such a direct call, i.e. osc_enqueue_base() still stores data in lock, thus we cannot take it from resource in llu

Thus we need old ldlm_inode_from_lock() and new function that get inode from resource. Unfortunately,  ldlm_inode_from_lock already used, so  ldlm_inode_from_resource looks like good choice for me.

Patch Set 1:

Jinshan, can you please comment on this when you get a chance.  I would prefer to have a simpler solution where the inode is only referenced from the resource, instead of increasing the complexity by having it referenced by both the lock and the resource.

Patch Set 1:

I'll take a look at asap.

Patch Set 1:

hmm.. is there any other concerns to store inode{} in ldlm_resource{} other than fixing this bug?

To be honest, I don't like this idea very much because it changes the semantic of md_set_lock_data(). This will cause problem if a new type of MD lock is defined and we want to set a specific private data for that kind of lock. Furthermore, ldlm_resource is a sharing resource so any attempts to store a lock type specific data in it is not good.

Actually if you only want to fix this problem, isn't it easier to check if l_ast_data is NULL before clearing the bit in ll_have_md_lock()?

Patch Set 1:

>hmm.. is there any other concerns to store inode{} in ldlm_resource{}
>other than fixing this bug?
Another races are possible, because inode can be "connected" with some locks. If inode reference is stored in resource, then such races are impossible.
>To be honest, I don't like this idea very much because it changes the
>semantic of md_set_lock_data(). This will cause problem if a new type of
>MD lock is defined and we want to set a specific private data for that kind
>of lock. Furthermore, ldlm_resource is a sharing resource so any attempts
>to store a lock type specific data in it is not good.
Patch change store method for mdc only. New type of MD lock will store data as it "want".
>Actually if you only want to fix this problem, isn't it easier to check if
>l_ast_data is NULL before clearing the bit in ll_have_md_lock()?
If l_ast_data is NULL then there is no information about inode. We can't call ll_have_md_lock()

Patch Set 1:

Previous message is terrible formatted. The same, but without quotes.
 
Another races are possible, because inode can be "connected" with some locks. If inode reference is stored in resource, then such races are impossible. 

Patch change store method for mdc only. New type of MD lock will store data as it "want". 

If l_ast_data is NULL then there is no information about inode. We can't call ll_have_md_lock()

Patch Set 1:

Yes, it's okay for new type of MD lock.

But from your explanation, the first lock(with NULL inode) should be canceled after the second lock; the 2nd lock's inode is not NULL so I think ll_have_md_lock() can be called.

Patch Set 1:

There is explanation here
https://bugzilla.lustre.org/show_bug.cgi?id=24555#c27

Patch Set 1:

>Can you please try if this patch can solve the problem?

Sorry, I can't reproduce bug in my workplace. Before we ask tester can we discuss this patch?

+                               if (lock->l_ast_data == inode)
+                                       *bits &= ~(lock->l_policy_data.l_inodebits.bits);

Yes, this solve problem with invalidating. Now we ignore locks with nulled inode. But lock with nulled inode, is still in granted queue. This adds some troubles:
 1. Another ldlm_lock_match callers (for example,  mdc_revalidate_lock) can find this lock with nulled last_data.
 2. Additional memory using
LU-903 patch make important cleanup. It moves inode reference to resource.  All locks that belong to same resource now have one reference to inode. This prevents from situations then references changed partly. Such situation was in 24555. Advantages:
 1. Memory using reducing.
 2. Resource is more appropriate place to store common data such inode. Such changes are already done in CLIO.
Yes, we could fix this issue another way. Try to fix synchronization for problem from 24555, but this patch fix problem and make code clear.

Patch Set 1:

Indeed, I'll take a look at this patch again.

Patch Set 1:

Jinshan, to be clear - I like the idea of moving the inode reference to the resource lr_lvb_inode (this is done on the OST already), since we should always have only a single inode per resource.  My main objection was to keep _both_ a reference on the DLM lock and the resource.  If we can come to a solution that has only a reference from the resource, that would be best, IMHO.

Patch Set 1:

Hi Andreas, if I understand it correctly, you're asking if we still need the field l_ast_data in ldlm_lock, is that right? We still need it because osc uses it to store a pointer to osc_lock.

Patch Set 1:

Hi Artem, can you please rebase your patch on current master? Thanks.

parents.subject: LU-2608 tests: check_catastrophe success on failure to connect

commit.subject: LU-903 ldlm: inode references moved to resource
commit.message:
LU-903 ldlm: inode references moved to resource

There is a race condition while get_attr after cancel_lru_locks
and sysctl drop_caches. ll_clear_inode clears l_ast_data for
ldlm lock and this lock can't be canceled because "inode == NULL".
ll_mdc_blocking_ast finds such lock. As result DCACHE_LUSTRE_INVALID
is not set and lookup returns wrong inode.

This patch moves inode structure reference to
"ldlm_lock::l_resource::lr_lvb_inode". This prevents from different
inode references for same resource's lock.

Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-338
Reviewed-by: Vitaly Fertman <vitaly_fertman@xyratex.com>
Reviewed-by: Alexey Lyashkov <alexey_lyashkov@xyratex.com>
Signed-off-by: Artem Blagodarenko <artem_blagodarenko@xyratex.com>
Change-Id: I4105b2aec38c90d3f5a20d1498a563192a74de55
